meta.title: ProxMenux — Menu-Driven Tool for Proxmox VE Management | Open Source
meta.description: Open-source, menu-driven tool for Proxmox VE management. ProxMenux is two pieces sharing one project: a CLI/TUI for post-install tweaks, VM and LXC creation, GPU and Coral TPU passthrough, disk, storage and network workflows; and a self-hosted web dashboard with proactive health monitoring, notifications to Telegram / Discord / Email, AI rewrite and a REST API.
meta.ogTitle: ProxMenux — Menu-Driven Tool for Proxmox VE Management
meta.ogDescription: Open-source CLI/TUI plus a web dashboard for Proxmox VE. Post-install, VMs, GPUs, disks, network, storage, security — and a separate Monitor with health checks, notifications, AI rewrite and REST API.
meta.twitterTitle: ProxMenux — Menu-Driven Tool for Proxmox VE Management
meta.twitterDescription: Open-source CLI/TUI + web dashboard for Proxmox VE. Health Monitor, notifications, AI rewrite, REST API.

header.title: Introduction
header.description: ProxMenux is an open-source, menu-driven tool for Proxmox VE. Two pieces share the same project: a CLI/TUI that wraps the most common host, VM, container, storage, network and security workflows; and a self-hosted web dashboard with proactive health monitoring, notifications, an AI rewrite hook and a REST API.

hero.tagline: One menu, every workflow. Everything that normally takes a tab open to the wiki, two forum threads and a copy-pasted command sequence — exposed as a numbered option you can run in seconds.
hero.audience: Built for first-time Proxmox users (safe, opinionated defaults) and experienced admins (fewer keystrokes for tasks they've done a hundred times). Free and open source under an active community on GitHub.

twoProducts.heading: Two products, one project
twoProducts.intro: ProxMenux ships as two independent pieces sharing the same source tree. The installer deploys both — they live side by side on the host and don't depend on each other:

scripts.title: ProxMenux Scripts
scripts.body: The original CLI/TUI you launch with menu. Nine functional areas covering post-install optimizations, VM and LXC creation, GPU and Coral TPU passthrough, disks, storage, network, security, utilities, plus a curated commands reference. Runs in any terminal — local, SSH or web.

monitor.title: ProxMenux Monitor
monitor.body: Self-hosted web dashboard (AppImage). Real-time host metrics, a proactive Health Monitor, notifications, an optional AI rewrite hook (six providers), an integrated web terminal and a REST API. Enable from the Scripts menu (Settings → ProxMenux Monitor).

twoProducts.calloutIntro: Three layers, all reachable from the same project:
layers: The Proxmox host — repos, packages, network, ZFS, Ceph, kernel, observability.
Virtual machines (VMs) — creation, GPU passthrough, OVA / OVF import / export, disk passthrough.
LXC containers — creation, mount points, GPU / TPU passthrough, NFS / Samba clients and servers.

scriptsSection.heading: ProxMenux Scripts — what you get
scriptsSection.intro: Nine functional areas, each with its own menu inside menu and a detail page in the documentation. Click any card to jump straight to that section's overview.

items.title: Post-Install
items.description: Optimizations for a freshly installed Proxmox setup: repos, kernel, time sync, basic security, performance, with reversible options.

items.title: GPUs & Coral TPU
items.description: NVIDIA, Intel iGPU, AMD and Coral TPU passthrough to VMs and LXC containers, with mode switching between VM and LXC.

items.title: Create VM
items.description: Wizards for NAS systems, Windows and Linux — with sensible defaults for hardware, storage and network.

items.title: Disk Manager
items.description: Import physical disks to VMs and LXC, format and wipe, SMART tests, qcow2 / vmdk / vdi image import — from a menu.

items.title: Storage & Share
items.description: Register NFS, Samba (CIFS), iSCSI and local storage in Proxmox; share folders between host and LXC containers via bind mounts or network protocols.

items.title: Network
items.description: Read-only diagnostics, analyze-then-suggest workflows and guided repairs for /etc/network/interfaces. Mandatory backups before any change.

items.title: Security
items.description: Fail2Ban (intrusion prevention for SSH and the web UIs) and Lynis (hardening audit) — install and manage with sensible defaults.

items.title: Utilities
items.description: Build Windows ISOs from UUP Dump, install CLI tools, run a safe system update, perform PVE 8 → 9 upgrade, export and import OVA / OVF VMs.

items.title: Commands Reference
items.description: Curated catalogue of Linux and Proxmox commands organised by topic — system, VM/CT (qm, pct), storage, network, ZFS, GPU passthrough, backup, day-to-day CLI tools.

monitorSection.heading: ProxMenux Monitor — what you get
monitorSection.intro: The web dashboard product. Self-hosted on the Proxmox host on TCP 8008. Click any card to jump straight to that section's page, or read the <link>Monitor overview</link> first.

items.title: Health Monitor
items.description: Ten categories scanned every 5 minutes (CPU, memory, storage, disks/SMART, network, VMs, services, logs, updates, security) with severity levels and per-category suppression.

items.title: Notifications
items.description: Telegram, Discord, Email, Gotify and Apprise (multi-channel) — with deduplication, cooldown, burst aggregation, quiet hours and a complete history.

items.title: AI Assistant
items.description: Optional rewrite of notifications in plain language. Six providers: OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, local Ollama. Twelve languages.

items.title: Web Terminal
items.description: Browser-based interactive shell on the Proxmox host plus a separate ProxMenux Scripts terminal that runs the menu through the web UI.

items.title: REST API
items.description: HTTP endpoints for system, health, storage / SMART, network, VMs, notifications and security — useful for Homepage, Home Assistant, Prometheus or custom scripts.

items.title: Integrations
items.description: Native Proxmox VE webhook, Prometheus scrape, Uptime Kuma probe, Homepage / Home Assistant / Glance widgets, n8n / Zapier flows, Tailscale Secure Gateway.

installPaths.heading: Install paths
installPaths.intro: The installer deploys Scripts and Monitor in one go. Scripts has two flavours — translation or lightweight — and the Monitor is installed as a standalone AppImage you enable from the menu later:

rows.bundles: CLI/TUI + Python venv + googletrans (English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese)
CLI/TUI English-only, smaller footprint, no Python venv
rows.when: Most users — Proxmox docs are English anyway

rows.bundles: Self-contained AppImage on TCP 8008. Independent install — can be enabled from the Scripts menu, or run the AppImage directly.
rows.when: When a web dashboard for the host is useful — phone, tablet, browser tab

installPaths.outro: Detailed install commands and prerequisites are on the <link>Installation page</link>.

warnSource.title: Always check what scripts you run from the internet
warnSource.body: ProxMenux is open source and every script is reviewable on GitHub. Before running the install command, look at the source. Do the same with any other automation tool.

items.tail: covers what it shows, how it's built and how to secure it.
is a curated catalog organised by topic, with copy buttons.
covers Coral TPU, NVIDIA passthrough, Kodi LXC and more.
